summary: A historical essay evaluating the life, legend, and mathematical contributions of Hypatia of Alexandria. The author concludes that while she was a highly revered and charismatic teacher, her mathematical work primarily consisted of editing and preserving existing knowledge (such as commentaries on Diophantus and Ptolemy) rather than producing original research.

principles.statement: Physical beauty and the material body are inherently shameful and unclean compared to higher philosophical ideals.
principles.rationale: She maintained determined celibacy and actively repelled physical lust, famously confronting a suitor with a menstrual pad to demonstrate the base, unclean nature of the physical body he desired.
